title: Did Henry Kissinger Oversee A UFO Crash Retrieval Program?
summary: This interview explores the theory that Henry Kissinger may have been involved in overseeing UFO crash retrieval programs, tracing his career from Counter Intelligence Corps work in post-WWII Germany through his rise in academia and government. The investigation examines connections between Kissinger's positions, Nazi scientist programs like Operation Paperclip, psychological warfare boards, and alleged UFO-related activities at facilities like Wright Patterson and Sandia Labs.

acronyms.definition: The Army's counterintelligence organization that played a key role in post-war Europe securing Nazi technology, hunting war criminals, and safeguarding military secrets
A high-level program launched by Truman designed to unify psychological warfare efforts across US agencies
A private organization tied to elite circles of business, government, and academia that served as a discussion club for powerful leaders in foreign affairs
Allegedly a secret committee formed by President Truman in 1947 to handle the extraterrestrial issue, including top scientists, military leaders, and government officials

events.description: Nuclear bomb codenamed Harry was detonated at the Nevada test site during Operation Upshot Knothole, allegedly causing a UFO crash near Kingman, Arizona
Henry Kissinger was one of the first officers to occupy the barracks at Oberammergau, a location with secret Nazi weapons facilities
Henry Kissinger enrolled at Harvard while maintaining ties to the Counter Intelligence Corps
CIA memos documented national security concerns about flying saucers and over 1,500 UFO reports
New York Herald Tribune published article about US scientists aiming to conquer gravity, with research sanctioned by Gordon Gray at University of North Carolina
Chapel Hill Conference held at Institute of Field Physics convening world's top theoretical physicists to discuss gravity, sponsored by Wright Airfield
President Kennedy requested a full briefing on MJ12 activities from CIA director Alan Dulles regarding cold war psychological warfare
Bluegill Triple Prime nuclear test in the Pacific allegedly downed a UFO, with craft recovered and sent to Albuquerque operations office
Kissinger became National Security Adviser under President Nixon

key_claims.claim: Maria Wang confirmed that her late husband Eric Wang worked on classified projects at Wright Patterson involving technology not of this world and that he reported directly to Henry Kissinger
CIC agents interrogated Victor Schauberger for nine months and considered his anti-gravity discoveries more important than Nazi nuclear research
Donald Menzel ordered the destruction of Harvard's astronomical photographic plates taken during the 1952 Washington DC UFO flap
Richard Bissell briefed Harold Malmgren on other-worldly technologies
A declassified document from 2022 JFK files confirms meetings between Dulles and JFK on June 28, 1961, corroborating a date mentioned in MJ12 documents released in 1995
Edward Teller was working with others on the objective to control gravity according to Australian intelligence
Lawrence Preston Gise was directly involved in reverse engineering recovered craft in the early 1960s
Nixon took Jackie Gleason to see alien bodies at a secret facility
